source_name: Virginia JLARC Report 700 — Data Center Tax Exemptions
access_path: https://jlarc.virginia.gov — Reports → 2024 → search data center
notes: $437M FY2023 foregone revenue figure. Legislative audit quality. Extract: annual cost trend, methodology, net fiscal impact analysis.

source_name: Blue Owl Capital BDC 10-K — Portfolio Investment Schedule
access_path: EDGAR CIK 0001655888 → latest 10-K → portfolio investment schedule. URL: https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar?action=getcompany&CIK=0001655888&type=10-K
notes: Look for Beignet Investor LLC or related entity in the portfolio schedule. If present: principal amount, fair value, yield, investment type (debt/equity). This is the primary evidence for the SPV structure.

source_name: IURC eDocket — Indiana Michigan Power 2024 IRP (Cause 46090)
access_path: https://iurcportal.in.gov → Case Search → Company: Indiana Michigan Power → filter IRP → Cause 46090
notes: Extract: data center load forecast (GW), generation resource commitments to serve new load, gas vs. renewable mix, explicit mention of Project Rainier or large data center customers. Also search Cause 46090 for staff reports.

source_name: IURC eDocket — Data Center / Large Load keyword search
access_path: https://iurcportal.in.gov → Case Search → keyword: data center OR large load → filter by Indiana Michigan Power
notes: Note any cause numbers referencing data centers or large load interconnection. May surface CPCN filings for generation to serve Rainier campus.

source_name: FERC eLibrary — EC Dockets (PE Utility Acquisitions 2015–2024)
access_path: https://elibrary.ferc.gov/eLibrary/search → Docket Type: EC → date range 2015–2024 → export list
notes: Count and classify fund-backed utility acquisitions. Verify or build on the Senate Budget Committee's '91 acquisitions' figure. Note: search for 'private equity' or 'fund' in the purchaser description.

source_name: Good Jobs First — Amazon Indiana Subsidy Records
access_path: https://subsidytracker.goodjobsfirst.org → search Amazon + Indiana → filter property tax abatement
notes: Individual deal records for Project Rainier campus. Cross-check against IEDC records. Note their advocacy posture; use data not conclusions.

source_name: Good Jobs First — Amazon Virginia Subsidy Records
access_path: https://subsidytracker.goodjobsfirst.org → search Amazon + Virginia
notes: Cross-reference with JLARC Rpt700 aggregate. Bottom-up deal-level check.

source_name: McKinsey State of AI 2025 Annual Survey
access_path: https://mckinsey.com → Insights → Artificial Intelligence → State of AI. Free with email registration.
notes: Retrieve exact question wording for the '80% see no bottom-line impact' claim before citing it. The question framing matters — adoption vs. financial return are different surveys. Note methodology: proprietary panel, social desirability bias.

source_name: Fitch/Moody's Rating Press Release — CoreWeave Debt
access_path: https://fitchratings.com → Research → search CoreWeave. Also: https://moodys.com → Research → CoreWeave. Free press releases only (full reports paywalled).
notes: Extract: leverage ratios, debt structure summary, key risk factors (customer concentration, technology obsolescence). This is the primary source for neocloud stranded-asset risk argument.

source_name: FT — Blue Owl / Beignet Data Center Financing Article (Oct 2024)
access_path: Search FT.com for 'Blue Owl data center' or 'Beignet Investor LLC'. Try: (1) 3 free monthly articles if available; (2) archive.ph search for FT URL; (3) Reuters/Bloomberg confirmation of key facts.
notes: Primary reconstruction of the SPV deal structure: Meta → Beignet Investor LLC → Blue Owl Capital (80%) → Pimco bond investors → maturity 2049. Note: Meta exit option 2033. If FT unavailable, Bloomberg confirmed key facts — use Bloomberg as citation.

source_name: FT — CoreWeave Financing Series (2023–2024)
access_path: Search FT.com for 'CoreWeave' filtered by 2023-2024. archive.ph often captures these. Key facts also confirmed by Bloomberg and Reuters.
notes: Nvidia equity stake in CoreWeave, $7.5B term loan context, Microsoft customer concentration background. Note: S-1 is now the primary source for most of these facts — FT reporting is corroborating context.

source_name: Indiana IEDC Board Minutes — Amazon/Boone County Tax Credits
access_path: https://iedc.in.gov → About → Board Meetings → 2022–2024 minutes. Or: Programs → Tax Credits → search Amazon.
notes: Look for IEDC conditional tax credit approval for Amazon Project Rainier. Should specify: investment commitment, projected employment, credit type, term. Anchors the $8B subsidy figure to a specific government approval document.

source_name: Boone County Assessor — Statement of Benefits (SB-1) Forms
access_path: Boone County Assessor, Indiana. Address: 121 N. Capitol Ave, Lebanon IN 46052. Phone: (765) 482-0940. Form SB-1 filings are public records. May require direct contact or in-person request under Indiana APRA.
notes: SB-1 discloses: investment amount, projected employment, abatement value by year, abatement duration. Primary quantification of the property tax subsidy. Cross-check with Good Jobs First figure.

source_name: Senate Budget Committee — PE Utility Acquisitions Report (Oct 2024)
access_path: https://budget.senate.gov → Reports and Documents → search 'utility' or 'private equity' → 2024. Also: https://govinfo.gov → Congressional Reports → Senate Budget.
notes: The '91 fund-backed utility acquisitions' figure source. If the formal report is not found here, it may be a majority staff report (not formally voted) — check the press release tab on budget.senate.gov for the October 2024 release.

source_name: Indiana DNR — Amazon Dewatering Permit (Boone County)
access_path: Indiana DNR Division of Water. Submit APRA request: in.gov/dnr → Contact → Div. of Water. Request: temporary dewatering permit for Amazon Data Services in Boone County, 2022–2024. Response within 24 hours under Indiana APRA.
notes: Verify the '2.2M gallons/hour for 730 days' figure. This is a striking number and should be confirmed against the actual permit before citing analytically. State investigating dry neighbor wells — the permit or subsequent enforcement action may document this.

source_name: Army Corps of Engineers — Section 404 Permit (Louisville District)
access_path: https://lrl.usace.army.mil → Regulatory → Permits → Public Notices. Search Boone County Indiana, 2022–2024. Also: RIBITS for wetland mitigation bank credits: https://ribits.usace.army.mil
notes: Verify the '10-acre wetland' impact claim. Public Notice would show: project footprint, wetland delineation, proposed fill acreage, mitigation plan. If Army Corps permit required, this is primary evidence for the community externality claim.

source_name: Delaware Corp Search — Beignet Investor LLC
access_path: https://corp.delaware.gov → Get Corporation Information → search 'Beignet Investor'. $10 fee for status report.
notes: Confirms LLC existence, incorporation date, registered agent. Anchors the SPV timeline relative to Meta's announced capex buildout. The actual operating agreement is not public.

source_name: PJM Interconnection Queue — Active Demand Resources Download
access_path: https://www.pjm.com/-/media/planning/new-services-queue/queued-projects-details.ashx — Excel download, no registration.
notes: Filter for demand resources (load-side) in AEP zone (covers Indiana). May show Project Rainier as a queued load under a project number. Individual identities may be masked in public data.

source_name: LBNL Data Center Energy Usage Report (Dec 2024)
access_path: https://www.osti.gov/biblio/2431240 or https://escholarship.org/uc/item/6gq8m7rr
notes: RETRIEVED by researcher agent. Key figures: 176 TWh (2023 actual); 325-580 TWh projected 2028. Cite as: Shehabi et al. (2024), LBNL-2001524. Add to source_citations.csv: lbnl_dc_energy_2023_twh=176, lbnl_dc_2028_low=325, lbnl_dc_2028_high=580.

source_name: CoreWeave S-1/A Registration Statement (Mar 2025)
access_path: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/EDGAR/data/2051911/000119312525062219/ds1a.htm
notes: RETRIEVED by researcher agent. Key: Microsoft ~62% of 2024 revenue; $15.1B RPO; $8B debt at 11-12.5%; take-or-pay language is 'may include' not absolute. Google NOT prominently in customer concentration data.

source_name: FERC Docket AD24-11 — Policy Statement (May 2024)
access_path: https://www.ferc.gov/sites/default/files/2024-05/AD24-11-000.pdf
notes: RETRIEVED by researcher agent. Status: non-binding Policy Statement only — no NOPR or Final Rule yet. Para 15: cost-causation applies to large loads. Para 22: upgrade costs 'may be inappropriately socialized' across ratepayers. Para 45: AI data centers present 'novel challenges'.

source_name: FERC Order 2023 — Generator Interconnection Reform
access_path: https://www.ferc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-07/E-1_0.pdf
notes: RETRIEVED by researcher agent. NOTE: This governs GENERATOR interconnection (supply-side), NOT large load/data center interconnection. Use for generator queue reform context only. Use AD24-11 for data center cost allocation claims.

source_name: Acemoglu NBER WP 32487 — Simple Macroeconomics of AI (May 2024)
access_path: https://www.nber.org/papers/w32487
notes: RETRIEVED. Free substitute for paywalled Goldman Sachs note. Same argument: AI investment exceeds plausible near-term productivity returns. Peer-reviewed, Nobel laureate author. Preferred citation over GS note.
